---
title: "Beaches_Bikes_Weather"
author: "Derek Kusmenko"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

## Importing Data and Libraries

```{r}
library(opendatatoronto)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(leaflet)
library(skimr)
library(janitor)
library(lubridate)
library(tidytext)
library(dplyr)
library(broom)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
df <- read.csv("../01_Data/beaches_bikes.csv")
```

## Bike/Weather Analysis

```{r}

Toronto_Daily_Temp <- read_csv("../01_Data/Toronto_Daily_Temp.csv")
Toronto_Daily_Temp <- clean_names(Toronto_Daily_Temp)
Toronto_Daily_Temp <- Toronto_Daily_Temp |> filter(local_year > 2020)

# parse date so it is compatible with the bike share data
temp_clean <- Toronto_Daily_Temp |> 
  mutate(
    
    date = as_date(local_date),

    month_name = month(date, label = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  # sort by the new datetime column
  arrange(date) |> 

  filter(!is.na(date))

```

```{r}
# join temperature data to bike share
combined_data <- df |>
  mutate(join_date = as_date(end_date_actual)) |> 
  inner_join(temp_clean, by = c("join_date" = "date")) |> 
  mutate(year = year(join_date))

```

```{r}
# Prepare the daily data
daily_data_by_type <- combined_data %>%
  group_by(start_date_actual, mean_temperature, User.Type) %>%
  summarise(
    total_trips = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# plot with Facets
ggplot(daily_data_by_type, aes(x = mean_temperature, y = total_trips)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "darkblue") + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red") +   
  
  facet_wrap(~ User.Type, scales = "free_y") + 
  
  labs(
    title = "Bike Trips vs. Temperature (Faceted)",
    subtitle = "Comparing rider behavior side-by-side",
    x = "Mean Temperature (°C)",
    y = "Total Daily Trips"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
# Prepare the daily data
filtered_daily_data <- combined_data %>%
  # Aggregate to daily counts per user type
  group_by(start_date_actual, mean_temperature, User.Type) %>%
  summarise(total_trips = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  
  # filter for shoulder season
  filter(month(start_date_actual) %in% c(1, 2, 3, 10, 11, 12))

# plot
ggplot(filtered_daily_data, aes(x = mean_temperature, y = total_trips)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ User.Type, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Bike Trips vs. Temperature (Cold Season Only)",
    subtitle = "Months: Jan-Mar, Oct-Dec",
    x = "Mean Temperature (°C)",
    y = "Total Daily Trips"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
# run correlation test for each group
correlation_results <- filtered_daily_data %>%
  group_by(User.Type) %>%
  summarise(

    correlation = cor(mean_temperature, total_trips, use = "complete.obs"),
    
    p_value = cor.test(mean_temperature, total_trips)$p.value,

    n_obs = n()
  )

# show results
print(correlation_results)
```

### Using 3 Clusters

Compare 3 clusters rather than user type

```{r}
set.seed(2453)
# clean data
clustering_temp <- combined_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(Trip..Duration) & !is.na(start_hour_of_day)) %>%
  filter(Trip..Duration < 7200)

kmeans_scaled <- scale(clustering_temp %>% select(Trip..Duration, start_hour_of_day))
trip_clusters <- kmeans(kmeans_scaled, centers = 3, nstart = 25)
clustering_temp$Cluster <- as.factor(trip_clusters$cluster)

# prepare dily data
daily_data_cluster <- clustering_temp %>%
  # Group by date, temperature, and Cluster
  group_by(start_date_actual, mean_temperature, Cluster) %>%
  summarise(total_trips = n(), .groups = "drop")


# Summarise the profiles including User Type Proportions
cluster_profiles <- clustering_temp |>
  group_by(Cluster) |>
  summarise(
    Total_Trips = n(),
    Avg_Start_Hour = round(mean(start_hour_of_day), 1),
    Avg_Duration_Mins = round(mean(Trip..Duration) / 60, 1),
    
    # Calculate the percentage of each member type
    Pct_Annual = round(sum(User.Type == "Annual Member") / n() * 100, 1),
    Pct_Casual = round(sum(User.Type == "Casual Member") / n() * 100, 1)
  ) |>
  arrange(Cluster)

# Print the resulting table
print(cluster_profiles)

# plot with facets by cluster
ggplot(daily_data_cluster, aes(x = mean_temperature, y = total_trips)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "darkblue") + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red") +  
  
  facet_wrap(~ Cluster, scales = "free_y", 
             labeller = as_labeller(c("1" = "Cluster 1: Leisure", 
                                      "2" = "Cluster 2: Morning Trips", 
                                      "3" = "Cluster 3: Evening Trips"))) + 
  
  labs(
    title = "Bike Trips vs. Temperature by Rider Persona",
    subtitle = "Analyzing how different clusters respond to weather changes",
    x = "Mean Temperature (°C)",
    y = "Total Daily Trips"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}

set.seed(2453)
# clean data
clustering_temp <- combined_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(Trip..Duration) & !is.na(start_hour_of_day)) %>%
  filter(Trip..Duration < 7200)

kmeans_scaled <- scale(clustering_temp %>% select(Trip..Duration, start_hour_of_day))
trip_clusters <- kmeans(kmeans_scaled, centers = 3, nstart = 25)
clustering_temp$Cluster <- as.factor(trip_clusters$cluster)

# prepare data for shoulder season
filtered_daily_data_cluster <- clustering_temp %>%
  # Filter for shoulder season
  filter(month(start_date_actual) %in% c(1, 2, 3, 10, 11, 12)) %>%
  # Group by date, temperature, and Cluster
  group_by(start_date_actual, mean_temperature, Cluster) %>%
  summarise(total_trips = n(), .groups = "drop")


# Summarise the profiles including User Type Proportions
cluster_profiles <- clustering_temp |>
  filter(month(start_date_actual) %in% c(1, 2, 3, 10, 11, 12)) %>%
  group_by(Cluster) |>
  summarise(
    Total_Trips = n(),
    Avg_Start_Hour = round(mean(start_hour_of_day), 1),
    Avg_Duration_Mins = round(mean(Trip..Duration) / 60, 1),
    
    # Calculate the percentage of each member type
    Pct_Annual = round(sum(User.Type == "Annual Member") / n() * 100, 1),
    Pct_Casual = round(sum(User.Type == "Casual Member") / n() * 100, 1)
  ) |>
  arrange(Cluster)

# Print the resulting table
print(cluster_profiles)

# plot result
ggplot(filtered_daily_data_cluster, aes(x = mean_temperature, y = total_trips)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Cluster, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = as_labeller(c("1" = "Cluster 1: Leisure", 
                                      "2" = "Cluster 2: Morning Trips", 
                                      "3" = "Cluster 3: Evening Trips"))) + 
  labs(
    title = "Bike Trips vs. Temperature (Cold Season Only)",
    subtitle = "Faceted by Rider Persona (Cluster)",
    x = "Mean Temperature (°C)",
    y = "Total Daily Trips"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
# run the correlation test for each cluster group
correlation_results_cluster <- filtered_daily_data_cluster %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    correlation = cor(mean_temperature, total_trips, use = "complete.obs"),
    
    p_value = cor.test(mean_temperature, total_trips)$p.value,
    
    n_days = n()
  )

# show results
print(correlation_results_cluster)
```

### Only using Leisure Cluster Data

```{r}
df2 <- read_csv("../01_Data/leisure_dataset.csv")
# join temperature data to bike share leisure cluster
combined_data <- df2 |>
  mutate(join_date = as_date(end_date_actual)) |> 
  inner_join(temp_clean, by = c("join_date" = "date")) |> 
  mutate(year = year(join_date))

```

```{r}
# Prepare the daily data 
daily_data_by_type <- combined_data %>%
  group_by(start_date_actual, mean_temperature, User.Type) %>%
  summarise(
    total_trips = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# plot with facets
ggplot(daily_data_by_type, aes(x = mean_temperature, y = total_trips)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "darkblue") + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red") +  
  
  facet_wrap(~ User.Type, scales = "free_y") + 
  
  labs(
    title = "Bike Trips vs. Temperature (Faceted)",
    subtitle = "Comparing rider behavior side-by-side",
    x = "Mean Temperature (°C)",
    y = "Total Daily Trips"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
# Prepare the daily data
filtered_daily_data <- combined_data %>%
  # Aggregate to daily counts per user type
  group_by(start_date_actual, mean_temperature, User.Type) %>%
  summarise(total_trips = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  
  #filter for shoulder season
  filter(month(start_date_actual) %in% c(1, 2, 3, 10, 11, 12))

# plot results
ggplot(filtered_daily_data, aes(x = mean_temperature, y = total_trips)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ User.Type, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Bike Trips vs. Temperature (Cold Season Only)",
    subtitle = "Months: Jan-Mar, Oct-Dec",
    x = "Mean Temperature (°C)",
    y = "Total Daily Trips"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
# Run the correlation test for each group
correlation_results <- filtered_daily_data %>%
  group_by(User.Type) %>%
  summarise(
    correlation = cor(mean_temperature, total_trips, use = "complete.obs"),
    
    p_value = cor.test(mean_temperature, total_trips)$p.value,
    
    n_obs = n()
  )

# show results
print(correlation_results)
```
